1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
239

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.

42	### Decision 1: Does `Classical.choose` extract data accessibly inside a `noncomputable def` body?
43	
44	- **Kernel-level fact** (`Init.Classical`, line 19–32 of the toolchain
45	  source at
46	  `/home/archon/.elan/toolchains/leanprover--lean4---v4.30.0-rc2/src/lean/Init/Classical.lean`):
47	
48	  ```
49	  noncomputable def indefiniteDescription {α : Sort u} (p : α → Prop) (h : ∃ x, p x) : {x // p x} :=
50	    choice <| let ⟨x, px⟩ := h; ⟨⟨x, px⟩⟩
51	
52	  noncomputable def choose {α : Sort u} {p : α → Prop} (h : ∃ x, p x) : α :=
53	    (indefiniteDescription p h).val

56	  So `Classical.choose h` is implemented as `(Classical.choice
57	  ⟨⟨x, px⟩⟩).val` — at the kernel level it is **just as opaque** as
58	  `Classical.choice`. There is no reduction rule

60	  `Mathlib.Logic.Basic` is a *propositional* equality, not definitional.)

63	  about `Classical.choose h = x` reducing definitionally. The defect is
64	  about the **outer expression structure** of the body.

69	    `Classical.choice` applied to a `Nonempty (ModuleCat k)`, so the
70	    body **as a term** does not reveal `(extendScalars _).obj _` to
71	    `unfold`/`whnf`. Downstream lemmas see only "some opaque element of
72	    `ModuleCat k`".

372	## Caveat — `unfold` exposure of `let`-bindings
373	
374	The closure chain depends on `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
375	exposing the `let`-bound `U, V, e, ψV` and the outer `extendScalars`
376	form so the rank lemma can `set V := h.choose_spec.choose` and apply
377	`Module.finrank_baseChange` to the resulting `(extendScalars …).obj M`.
378	
379	The risk: Lean's `unfold` may leave the `let`-bindings as
380	`Lean.Expr.letE` nodes rather than substituting them. In practice,
381	`simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity]` (or
382	`show (ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj _ = …` after a sequence of
383	`set`s) resolves this. The prover lane should **smoke-test** step 1
384	of the rank-lemma closure (`unfold + obtain + show`) before
385	committing to the full chain. If `unfold` does not expose cleanly,
386	the fallback is to add a small bridge lemma:
387	
388	```lean
389	lemma cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : …) :
390	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ …)
391	      (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ (η[G].left.appLE V ⊤ _)) …,
392	      cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G =
393	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars (… ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ ΓSpecIso _ …).hom).obj
394	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
395	  refine ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, ?_⟩
396	  rfl
397	```
398	
399	This bridge lemma — provable by `rfl` if the body is pure-term — would
400	serve as a stable interface for downstream consumers (rank lemma,
401	piece (i.b) globalisation). **Recommended** as a defensive complement
402	to the body refactor, regardless of whether `unfold` exposes cleanly.
403

140	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
141	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
142	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
143	
144	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The structural
145	properties (`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`, rank `= n`) are content
146	for the iter-132+ rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`; the
147	structural-shape accessibility needed by that lemma is witnessed here by
148	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`. -/
149	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
150	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
151	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
152	    ModuleCat k :=
153	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
154	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
155	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
156	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
157	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
158	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
159	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
160	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
161	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
162	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
163	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
164	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
165	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
166	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
167	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
168	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
169	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
170	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
171	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
172	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
173	    exact hxV
174	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
175	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
176	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
177	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
178	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
179	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
180	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
181	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
182	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
183	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
184	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
185	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
186	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
187	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
188	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
189	
190	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
191	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
192	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but
193	the outer `extendScalars`/`ModuleCat.of`-of-`Ω` form is exposed. This proves
194	that the iter-131 body refactor closed the iter-130 opacity defect: a
195	downstream rank lemma can `simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars]`
196	(combined with a `Classical.choose`-chain `obtain` on the same existential)
197	to rewrite the body to the explicit form. -/
198	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
200	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
201	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
202	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
203	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
204	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
205	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
206	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
207	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
208	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
209	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
210	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
211	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
212	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
213	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
214	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
215	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
216	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
217	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
218	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
219	  exact hxV
220	
221	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
222	
223	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
224	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
225	`finrank` equal to `n`.
226	
227	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
228	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
229	
230	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
231	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
232	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
233	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
234	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
235	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
236	
237	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
238	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
239	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
240	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
241	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
242	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
243	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
244	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
245	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
246	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
247	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
248	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
249	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
250	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
251	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
252	  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
253	  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
254	  set e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
255	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
256	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
257	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
258	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
259	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
260	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
261	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
262	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
263	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
264	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
265	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
266	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
267	    exact hxV
268	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
269	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
270	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
271	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
272	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
273	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
274	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
275	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
276	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
277	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
278	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
279	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
280	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
281	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
282	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
283	
284	end AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj

15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	/-- The Kähler module of a localization is subsingleton.
69	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. -/
70	theorem kaehler_localization_subsingleton
71	    {A L : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [Algebra A L]
72	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
73	    Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) :=
74	  letI : Algebra.FormallyUnramified A L :=
75	    Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization (R := A) (Rₘ := L) M
76	  Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential
77	
78	/-- Tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv` for the Kähler module under a localization:
79	if `A → L` is a localization at `M ⊆ A` and `L → B` is a ring map forming a
80	scalar tower `A → L → B`, then the canonical map `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/L]` is a
81	`B`-linear equivalence.
82	
83	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` and the most
84	extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1 (candidate name
85	`KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`). -/
86	noncomputable def kaehler_quotient_localization_iso
87	    {A L B : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [CommRing B]
88	    [Algebra A L] [Algebra A B] [Algebra L B] [IsScalarTower A L B]
89	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
90	    Ω[B⁄A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄L] := by
91	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) := kaehler_localization_subsingleton M
92	  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective (KaehlerDifferential.map A L B B) ⟨?_, ?_⟩
93	  · rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
94	    intro x hx
95	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ :=
96	      (KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map A L B x).mp hx
97	    -- y : B ⊗[L] Ω[L⁄A]; since Ω[L⁄A] is subsingleton, the tensor product is too
98	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A])) := by
99	      refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
100	      have hzero : ∀ z : TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A]), z = 0 := by

\begin{lemma}[(i.b) Shear-iso globalisation of the cotangent]
  \label{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight_globalises_cotangent}
  % Lean signature stub (iter-133 blueprint-writer hardening per the iter-133
  % mathlib-analogist verdict at
  % `task_results/mathlib-analogist-mulright-globalises-iter133.md` Decision 4
  % and the persistent design file `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`;
  % iter-134+ prover-lane target). The RHS is stated at the
  % `PresheafOfModules`-of-`G.left` level using the abstract pullback
  % presheaf `η_G^* (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)`, NOT at the
  % `ModuleCat k`-valued `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` level. Decoupling
  % piece (i.b)'s body from chart localisation in this way keeps the lemma
  % in the 210--440 LOC envelope; the chart-localisation bridge from this
  % sheaf-level RHS to `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is a separate
  % ~100--200 LOC chart-localisation identification consumed inside
  % piece (i.c) (`omega_free`), NOT inside this lemma's body:
  %   noncomputable def mulRight_globalises_cotangent
  %       (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G]
  %       {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
  %       relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅
  %         (PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)).obj
  %           ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_η G)).obj
  %             (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))
  % where:
  %   - `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is the Mathlib functor
  %     (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback`, signature
  %     `(PresheafOfModules S) ⥤ (PresheafOfModules R)` parameterised by a
  %     compatibility morphism `φ : S ⟶ F.op.comp R`).
  %   - `φ_str` packages the compatibility morphism for the structure map
  %     `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec (.of k)`; `φ_η` the compatibility morphism for
  %     `η[G].left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left`. Both are obtained via the
  %     `TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` of `CommRingCat`-valued
  %     structure presheaves, in the same shape as the `φ'` on
  %     `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:52`.
  %   - The home category is `G.left.PresheafOfModules`. The conclusion
  %     `Iso` is inside `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf` (the
  %     presheaf-of-modules-level statement; NOT the sheafified
  %     `SheafOfModules`-level version, which would require an extra
  %     sheafification step not needed for piece (i.b)).
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj, lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}
  \notready
  Let $\mu \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$ denote the multiplication of $G$ and let $\sigma \colon G \times_{k} G \to G \times_{k} G$ be the \emph{shear isomorphism}
  \[
    \sigma \;=\; \langle \pr_{1},\, \mu \rangle, \qquad (a, b) \;\mapsto\; (a,\, a \cdot b),
  \]
  built categorically from $\mu$ and the binary-product projections in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$. Then $\sigma$ is an isomorphism over the first projection, and pullback along $\sigma$ together with the canonical first-projection-cotangent identification $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \cong \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ (\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}) yields a natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on $G$
  \[
    \Omega_{G/k} \;\cong\; \pi_{G}^{*}\!\bigl(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}\bigr) \qquad \text{on } G,
  \]
  where $\pi_{G} \colon G \to \Spec k$ is the structure map (in the standard category-theory notation for the binary product in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, this is the "first projection" of the singleton-product-with-base, sometimes written $\pr_{1}$; we use $\pi_{G}$ to avoid confusion with the binary-product first projection $\pr_{1} \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$ appearing above). Informally: the relative cotangent of $G$ is canonically isomorphic to the constant $\mathcal O_{G}$-module with fibre $\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$, trivialised along the structure map.

  \paragraph{Iter-127 over-k risk register.} The globalisation \textbf{must} be formulated functorially via the shear scheme map $\sigma$ — which is a morphism in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, valid for any base field — and \textbf{not} as a pointwise translation by closed (or $\bar k$-rational) points of $G$. The pointwise-translation argument requires either alg-closure of $k$ or a Nullstellensatz-style $\bar k$-point lifting (as in Mathlib's \texttt{pointEquivClosedPoint}, used in the alg-closed-base branch of \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth.lean:50--51}); the functorial shear formulation sidesteps that obstacle entirely. The iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict (\texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 4) explicitly rules out the pointwise idioms of \texttt{Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra.mulInvariantVectorField} (requires \texttt{[Group G]}) and \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Group.Basic.Homeomorph.shearMulRight} (requires \texttt{[Group G]} on the carrier) as the over-k-forbidden alternatives.

  \paragraph{Lean target name vs.\ construction (option (i), iter-133).} The named target \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent} retains the \texttt{mulRight} naming convention because the shear iso $\sigma = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \rangle$ on $G \times_{k} G$ is the binary-product upgrade of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281}, which packages a one-input automorphism $a \mapsto a \cdot f$ for a fixed global element $f \colon \mathbf{1} \to G$): the map $\sigma$ parametrises the family of right-translations $\{\mathrm{mulRight}\,a\}_{a \in G}$ globally across $G$ by taking the first coordinate as the parameter, and the second projection $\pr_{2} \circ \sigma = \mu$ realises the right-translation action on the second coordinate. The "globalises the cotangent" phrasing in the name records that this family-of-translations is what triviallises $\Omega_{G/k}$ via pullback along $\sigma$ (cf.\ the inverse-pair proof shape of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback}, \texttt{Grp\_.lean:293--323}, which uses the structurally identical \texttt{lift \dots (lift \dots \,\iota\, \dots) ≫ μ} construction). No standalone \texttt{mulRight}-name function appears in the Lean signature itself; the connection is purely nomenclatural, and the underlying Lean construction is the binary-product shear iso, not a one-input \texttt{mulRight} automorphism.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj, lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}

  \emph{Step 1: Build the shear iso $\sigma$.} Define
  \[
    \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \;:=\; \langle \pr_{1},\, \mu \rangle \;=\; \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,μ} \colon G \times_{k} G \to G \times_{k} G
  \]
  in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ from the $\GrpObj$ data only. The candidate inverse
  \[
    \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \;:=\; \langle \pr_{1},\, \mu \circ (\iota \circ \pr_{1},\, \pr_{2}) \rangle \;=\; \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,(lift\,(fst\,G\,G\,≫\,$\iota$)\,(snd\,G\,G)\,≫\,μ)},
  \]
  where $\iota \colon G \to G$ is the inverse on $G$, is again a scheme map in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ — informally, $(a, b) \mapsto (a, a^{-1} \cdot b)$. One verifies $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} = \mathrm{id}_{G \times_{k} G} = \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$ using the $\GrpObj$ axioms (associativity and left/right inverse) via the Mathlib calculus \texttt{lift\_lift\_assoc}, \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_left}, \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_right}, and \texttt{comp\_lift\_assoc} — the same idioms used by \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} and \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281,\,293--323}). Estimated build: $\sim 30$--$60$ LOC. This is a NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL (no packaged $A \otimes A \cong A \otimes A$ binary-product shear iso exists in Mathlib, although the building blocks all do; per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict Decision 1).

  \emph{Step 2: Base-change of differentials along $\sigma$.} Since $\sigma$ is an iso over the first projection $\pr_{1}$ (i.e.\ $\pr_{1} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \pr_{1}$, by construction of $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$ via the binary-product universal property), pullback along $\sigma$ on the relative cotangent of $G \times_{k} G$ viewed as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$ supplies a canonical natural iso of presheaves of modules on $G \times_{k} G$
  \[
    \Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G \,\text{via $\pr_{1}$}} \;\cong\; \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}}^{*}\,\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G \,\text{via $\pr_{1} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \pr_{1}$}} \;=\; \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}}^{*}\,\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}.
  \]
  Combining with \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj} (the load-bearing helper sub-lemma stating $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \cong \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$, NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC), the Step 1 shear iso transports the natural iso into the form
  \[
    \pr_{1}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k} \;\cong\; (\mu)^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k} \qquad \text{on } G \times_{k} G,
  \]
  where the LHS is "the cotangent of the first coordinate" (pulled back to $G \times_{k} G$ along $\pr_{1}$) and the RHS is "the cotangent pulled back through the multiplication" (along $\mu = \pr_{2} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$).

  \emph{Step 3: Restrict along the section $s := \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \eta_{G} \rangle \colon G \to G \times_{k} G$.} The section $s$ satisfies $\pr_{1} \circ s = \mathrm{id}_{G}$ and $\pr_{2} \circ s = \eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}$ in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, where $\pi_{G} \colon G \to \Spec k$ is the structure map. Applying $s^{*}$ to both sides of Step 2's iso:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item LHS becomes $s^{*}(\pr_{1}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}) \cong (\pr_{1} \circ s)^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k} = \mathrm{id}_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k} \cong \Omega_{G/k}$, via \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp} composed with \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackId} (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback}).
    \item RHS becomes $s^{*}(\mu^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$. After the Step 2 transport, this equals $s^{*}(\sigma_{\mathrm{inv}}^{*}(\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})) = (\sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \circ s)^{*}(\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$. Computing $\sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \circ s = \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \mu \circ (\iota \circ \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \eta_{G}) \rangle = \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \eta_{G} \rangle = s$ (using $\mu(g^{-1}, \eta_{G}) = g^{-1}$ — i.e.\ $\eta_{G}$ is the right identity — and $\iota(g)^{-1} = g$? Actually let's reread: in the formula $\mu \circ (\iota \circ \pr_{1}, \pr_{2})$ applied to $(a, b) = (a, \eta_{G})$, we get $\mu(a^{-1}, \eta_{G}) = a^{-1}$; this doesn't equal $b = \eta_{G}$ in general, so $\sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \circ s \neq s$ — instead, $\sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \circ s = \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \iota \rangle$, hmm; the more careful framing is: working with $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$ directly, we have $\pr_{2} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ s = \mu \circ s = \mu \circ (\mathrm{id}_{G}, \eta_{G}) = \mathrm{id}_{G}$ — so post-composing with $\pr_{2}$ recovers the $\eta_{G}$-cotangent via $\eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}$). Concretely, \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section} packages the relevant section-restriction-and-composition computation as a sub-lemma, applying \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp} to the section identity $\pr_{2} \circ s = \eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}$. The output of that helper is
    \[
      s^{*}\!\bigl(\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}\bigr) \;\cong\; \pi_{G}^{*}\!\bigl(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}\bigr).
    \]
  \end{itemize}
  Composing LHS-side and RHS-side identifications yields the displayed iso $\Omega_{G/k} \cong \pi_{G}^{*}(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$. Estimated build: $\sim 30$--$80$ LOC (per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict Decision 4, step 3 of the LOC envelope).

Step 2 (base-change of differentials): \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj} (chains \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} from \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct} with the presheaf-side \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} from \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}, plus the project's \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler}; NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC). \begin{lemma}[(i.b-helper) Base-change of differentials along the second projection]
  \label{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two}
  % Lean signature stub (iter-134+ NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL helper for

  %           (CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.fst : (G ⊗ G).left ⟶ G.left).⋯ ≅

  % where `(G ⊗ G).left` is the underlying scheme of the categorical product
  % `G ⊗ G` in `Over (Spec (.of k))` (equivalently, the fibre product
  % `G.left ×_{Spec k} G.left`), the LHS uses the first projection
  % `pr_1 : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` as the structure morphism for the relative cotangent
  % of `G ⊗ G` viewed as a `G`-scheme, and `φ_pr_two` is the compatibility
  % morphism for the second projection `pr_2 : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` in the same shape
  % as `Differentials.lean:52`'s `φ'`. Note: the LHS index `pr_1`-vs.-`pr_2`
  % asymmetry follows from the binary-product universal property in
  % `Over (Spec k)`: the relative cotangent for `pr_1` is dual to pulling
  % back the cotangent of `G/Spec k` along `pr_2`, via
  % `tensorKaehlerEquiv`'s `Algebra.IsPushout` square.
  \uses{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
  \notready
  Let $G : \Over\,(\Spec k)$ be as above, and consider the binary product $G \times_{k} G$ in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, with first and second projections $\pr_{1}, \pr_{2} \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$. View $G \times_{k} G$ as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$. Then there is a canonical natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on $G \times_{k} G$
  \[
    \Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \;\cong\; \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k},
  \]
  where the left-hand side is the relative cotangent of $G \times_{k} G$ viewed as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$, and the right-hand side is the pullback along $\pr_{2}$ of $\Omega_{G/k} = \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,G.\mathrm{hom}$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
  The construction chains two Mathlib idioms. On each affine chart $W = \Spec B$ of the source $G \times_{k} G$, the sections of both sides are Kähler-differential modules with appropriate base rings. The left-hand side $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$ over $W$ gives $\Omega_{B/B_{1}}$, where $B_{1}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{1}$ to $W$. The right-hand side $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ over $W$ gives $B \otimes_{B_{2}} \Omega_{B_{2}/k}$, where $B_{2}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{2}$. The binary-product universal property in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ supplies an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}\,$k$\,$B_{1}$\,$B_{2}$\,$B$ square (the two projections $B_{1}, B_{2} \to B$ exhibit $B$ as the tensor product $B_{1} \otimes_{k} B_{2}$ at chart level). The algebra-side base-change identity
  \[
    \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} \colon \;\; B \,\otimes_{B_{2}}\, \Omega_{B_{2}/k} \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\; \Omega_{B/B_{1}}
  \]
  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}; for an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout R S A B} square, yields $B \otimes_{A} \Omega[A/R] \simeq_{B} \Omega[B/S]$) gives the chart-level value identity. Promoting this chart-by-chart equivalence to a natural iso of presheaves of modules uses the presheaf-side functor \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) composed with the project-side chart-level value identity \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60}); naturality follows from naturality of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} and of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback}.

Presheaf-side: \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) and \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback}). Project-side: \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60--66}). This is the load-bearing piece of (i.b); without it, the shear iso of Step 1 of \cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}'s proof cannot be turned into a cotangent-level iso. Mathlib has no scheme-level relative cotangent sheaf at all (per \texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 2), so this lemma is a genuine NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL contribution candidate. Compatible upstream Mathlib PR-shape: a sheaf-of-modules version of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} promoting \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-of-charts to a presheaf-level natural iso.
\end{proof}

  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}

115	  The identity section $\eta_G$ is a canonical $k$-rational point of $G$ supplied by the $\GrpObj$ structure. The iter-131 Lean construction (Replacement (B), pure-term \texttt{noncomputable def}) realises $\mathfrak g^{\vee} = \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ as a chart-base-changed Kähler module: by the forward Jacobian criterion \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (already in the tree as \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}), there exists an affine chart $V \subseteq G$ around the image of the identity section $\eta_G(\mathrm{pt})$, with affine base chart $U \subseteq \Spec k$ and the structural ring map $\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(G, V)$ exhibiting \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} $n$ (and consequently $\Omega_{\Gamma(G,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(G, V)$-module of rank $n$ by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). The cotangent space at the identity is then defined as the base change to $k$ of this algebraic Kähler module along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained by restricting the identity section $\eta_G$ to $V$ and composing with the canonical isomorphism $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$:

118	  \]

      s^{*}\!\bigl(\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}\bigr) \;\cong\; \pi_{G}^{*}\!\bigl(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}\bigr).
    \]
  \end{itemize}
  Composing LHS-side and RHS-side identifications yields the displayed iso $\Omega_{G/k} \cong \pi_{G}^{*}(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$. Estimated build: $\sim 30$--$80$ LOC (per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict Decision 4, step 3 of the LOC envelope).

\emph{Step 2: Base-change of differentials.} View $G \times_{k} G$ as a $G$-scheme via the first projection $\pr_{1}$. The relative cotangent of this $G$-scheme, $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$, is canonically isomorphic to the pullback $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ via \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj} (the load-bearing helper sub-lemma, NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC):
  \[
    \Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \;\cong\; \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k} \qquad \text{on } G \times_{k} G.
  \]
  Since $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$ is an iso over $\pr_{1}$ (i.e.\ $\pr_{1} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \pr_{1}$, by construction of $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \rangle$ via the binary-product universal property), $\sigma$ acts as a self-iso of $G \times_{k} G$ as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$; functoriality of $\Omega_{(\cdot)/G}$ under such self-isos then re-routes the Step 2 iso through $\sigma$ to relate the differentials at the two projections, with $\pr_{2} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \mu$.

  \emph{Step 3: Restrict along the section $s := \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \eta_{G} \rangle \colon G \to G \times_{k} G$.} The section $s$ satisfies $\pr_{1} \circ s = \mathrm{id}_{G}$ and $\pr_{2} \circ s = \eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}$ in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, where $\pi_{G} \colon G \to \Spec k$ is the structure map. Applying $s^{*}$ to both sides of the Step 2 iso $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \cong \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item The LHS $s^{*}(\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G})$ is, by the $G$-scheme structure given by $\pr_{1}$ and the section identity $\pr_{1} \circ s = \mathrm{id}_{G}$, canonically isomorphic to $\Omega_{G/k}$ (restricting the relative cotangent of $G \times_{k} G$ over $G$ to a section of the $G$-projection recovers $\Omega_{G/k}$ — a standard relative-cotangent identification for a section of a smooth morphism). Mathlib-wise this combines \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp} with \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackId} on the composition $\pr_{1} \circ s = \mathrm{id}_{G}$.
    \item The RHS $s^{*}(\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$ is, by \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section} applied to the section identity $\pr_{2} \circ s = \eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}$, canonically isomorphic to $\pi_{G}^{*}(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$.
  \end{itemize}
  Composing the LHS and RHS identifications with the pulled-back Step 2 iso yields the displayed iso $\Omega_{G/k} \cong \pi_{G}^{*}(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$. Estimated build: $\sim 30$--$80$ LOC (per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict Decision 4, step 3 of the LOC envelope). The role of $\sigma$ in this proof is the Step 2 functorial book-keeping — it pins the "right-translation" interpretation of the trivialisation (the second-coordinate cotangent $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ is identified with the first-projection cotangent $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$ via Step 2's helper, and $\sigma$ realises the consistency of these two views as a single $G$-scheme structure on $G \times_{k} G$).
